member.name: T:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig
member.summary: Settings for the frame heap.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.TrackingMode
member.summary: Determines the degree that frame heap allocations are tracked in debug mode. The default is "detect leaks".

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.Management
member.summary: Determines the memory management strategy the Quantum heap will use. The default is "page-based".

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.PageSize
member.summary: Determines the size of a heap page. The default is 8 KiB.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.SegmentSize
member.summary: Determines the size of a heap segment. The default is 2 MiB.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.SegmentCapacity
member.summary: The maximum number of segments each heap can allocate in order to grow. The default is 32.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.PageShift
member.summary: Determines the size of a heap page (expressed as the N in 2^N). The default is 15 (32 KiB).

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.PageCount
member.summary: The number of pages to allocate for each heap. The default is 256.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.ExtraHeapCount
member.summary: The number of additional heaps to create (useful if creating frames that are managed by you instead of Quantum). The default is 0.

member.name: P:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.HeapCount
member.summary: The number of heaps to create.

member.name: M:Quantum.Allocator.FrameHeapConfig.Clone
member.summary: Clone the current config.

member.name: T:Quantum.Allocator.HeapManagement
member.summary: Determines the memory management strategy the Quantum heap will use.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapManagement.PageBasedLegacy
member.summary: The heap splits its memory into pages and then splits those pages into pools of fixed-size blocks. This heap cannot grow or allocate blocks larger than its page size. (This is the heap from Quantum version 3.0 and earlier.)

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapManagement.PageBased
member.summary: The heap splits its memory segments into pages and then splits those pages into pools of fixed-size blocks.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapManagement.BlockBased
member.summary: The heap splits its memory segments directly into variable-size blocks.

member.name: T:Quantum.Allocator.HeapTrackingMode
member.summary: Determines the degree that frame heap allocations are tracked in debug mode.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapTrackingMode.Disabled
member.summary: Heap allocations are not tracked.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapTrackingMode.DetectLeaks
member.summary: Heap allocations are tracked. A comparison between tracked and serialized values can detect memory leaks.

member.name: F:Quantum.Allocator.HeapTrackingMode.TraceAllocations
member.summary: Heap allocations are tracked, including the stack trace of each allocation. This mode can detect memory leaks and report the exact point in the code where the leaked memory was allocated from. WARNING: tracing allocations can be VERY slow. This mode is not recommended unless a memory leak is being actively debugged.

member.name: T:Quantum.Allocator.HeapPageSize
member.summary: Determines the size of a heap page.

member.name: T:Quantum.Allocator.HeapSegmentSize
member.summary: Determines the size of a heap segment. (Segments are large chunks of contiguous memory the heap allocates from the operating system.)

member.summary: Return the memory as-is.

member.name: T:Quantum.Map
member.summary: The Quantum map is a Quantum asset that contains information about the level and is used by the deterministic simulation to drive gameplay. Only one map can be loaded at a time.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.UserAsset
member.summary: An optional generic asset reference that the map can be associated with.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.Scene
member.summary: The Unity scene name is baked into the map. It's loaded automatically if Simulation.AutoLoadSceneFromMap is enabled.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.ScenePath
member.summary: The Unity scene path is baked into the map for convenience.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.SceneGuid
member.summary: The Unity scene guid is baked into the map for convenience.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.WorldSize
member.summary: The physics scene size in the bucketing axis. The broad phase is clamped by a bounding box of all physics entries between -WorldSize/2 to WorldSize/2. It is therefore crucial to ensure the world is big enough to encompass all entities. If an entity is outside the world, it will cost you performance as it is added to either the first or last bucket. Everything outside the bounding box is considered to be at the world's edge, from the physics engine perspective, which will result in collision candidates. In the non bucketing axis, the physics world is only limited by the value range of FP.UsableMin to FP.UsableMax.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.BucketsCount
member.summary: The amount of buckets used in the broad phase, which are resolved in parallel. Use a reasonable amount according to how many physics entries (colliders) you have. Too many buckets and the handling overhead increases without any performance gain because there are only few entries in each one; too few buckets and there will be an excessive amount of entries in each, slowing down the broad phase performance.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.BucketsSubdivisions
member.summary: Regular queries (overlaps and raycasts) use a stabbing approach for checking as few entries as possible in the buckets subdivisions. Tweak the number in accordance with the expected amount of entries and regular queries you perform. Too many subdivisions will add overhead without performance, while too few will result in queries taking longer to resolve, because they will have to check too many entries.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.BucketingAxis
member.summary: Physics entries are put into buckets according to their position in the bucketing axis.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.SortingAxis
member.summary: The queries in a bucket are sorted according to their position in the sorting axis.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.SceneMeshCellSize
member.summary: Defines the size of the cells into which the 3D triangle soup is divided. This number should be adapted based on how dense the meshes' triangles density to get a reasonable amount of triangles per cell. For better visualization enable related fields in the QuantumEditorSettings asset's Collider gizmos section. This will affect the performance of both the broad phase and regular queries. Use the Task Profiler to analyse the performance and find the most suitable number for your game.

member.name: F:Quantum.Map.TriangleMeshCellSize
member.summary: Obsolete. Use instead for representing the cell size with an FP value.

member.summary: Number of navmesh grid cells in y-dimension. Toggle Navmesh Grid or Navmesh Area gizmos to preview.

member.name: T:Quantum.NavMesh
member.summary: The asset object that contains a Quantum navigation mesh. The object loads an additional data file during the . This is because of size limitations when loading the data with Unity serialization.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.Name
member.summary: Name of the navmesh. Access the navmesh by name through .

member.summary: How the NavMesh should be serialized.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.RegionMaskBlockCount
member.summary: Used to migrate to a higher max region count. This number will limit the amount of regions blocks serialized. Default is 2 to migrate old projects. Should be set to to enable max region count. Navmesh must be re-baked after changing.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.DataAsset
member.summary: Asset that contains the actual binary navmesh data.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.GridSizeX
member.summary: The navmesh triangles are partitioned by simple 2D grid. This is the number of all cells on the x-axis. Derived from the map values during baking.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.GridSizeY
member.summary: The number of all grid cells on the y-axis. Derived from the map values during baking.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.GridNodeSize
member.summary: The number of units one grid cell has in both dimensions. Minimum 2. Must be an even number. Derived from the map values during baking.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMesh.WorldOffset
member.summary: The center of the map is the origin. The world offset represents the lower left corner and is copied from during the navmesh baking.
member.remarks: WorldOffset.X = -(GridSizeX * GridNodeSize) / 2

member.summary: Returns the y-component of the triangle at point p. Does not check if the point is actually on the XZ-dimensions of the triangle. Projects the exact XZ position and does not find the closet position inside the triangle.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindClosestTriangle(Quantum.FrameThreadSafe,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,System.Int32@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Finds the closest navmesh triangle at the position plus radius. Works in 2D and 3D. This method sets the mailboxing optimization internally which requires the frame object. Otherwise it's identical to . Caveat: The result is only a heuristic and not 100% accurate due to simplified calculations to reduce precision issues. For example: in 3D the input position is projected down instead of finding the correct closest position. The search can be performance heavy so carefully choose the radius.
member.param: Frame object to locate mailbox memory
Search radius around starting position. Use FP.0 to only search at the exact input position.
The corrected source position on the navmesh
member.returns: when a triangle was found

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindClosestTriangle(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,System.Int32@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Finds the closest navmesh triangle at the position plus radius. Works in 2D and 3D. This method sets the mailboxing optimization internally which requires the frame object. Otherwise it's identical to . Caveat: The result is only a heuristic and not 100% accurate due to simplified calculations to reduce precision issues. For example: in 3D the input position is projected down instead of finding the correct closest position. The search can be performance heavy so carefully choose the radius.
member.param: Frame object to locate mailbox memory
Search radius around starting position. Use FP.0 to only search at the exact input position.
The corrected source position on the navmesh
member.returns: when a triangle was found

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindClosestTriangle(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,System.Int32@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Quantum.MailboxSet8)
member.summary: Finds the closest navmesh triangle at the position plus radius. Works in 2D and 3D. Caveat: The result is only a heuristic and not 100% accurate due to simplified calculations to reduce precision issues. For example: in 3D the input position is projected down instead of finding the correct closest position. The search can be performance heavy so carefully choose the radius.
member.param: Search radius around starting position. Use FP.0 to only search at the exact input position (don't use for 3D).
The corrected source position on the navmesh
Optionally add a mailbox data structure to optimize the performance of search by filtering out already checked triangles.
member.returns: when a triangle was found

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindTriangle2D(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds the triangle at position (X,Z). This will not work in 3D as overlapping triangles may occur. Always use FindClosestTriangle() in 3D.
member.param: Use inclusive when you want include the border and the vertices of the triangle
member.returns: Triangle id or -1

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindClosestTriangleFast(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@)
member.summary: Searches the closest triangle to the input position by using the fallback triangle saved for each grid cell. Result is quite inaccurate. To use this API the navmesh generation must generate fallback triangles enabled by setting to something else than .
member.param: Triangle regions are checked if they are active
member.remarks: This is only available if the NavMesh has been baked with the option to calculate the center grid.
member.returns: if a triangle was found

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindRandomPointOnTriangle(System.Int32,Photon.Deterministic.RNGSession*,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Generates a random point on the triangle.
member.param: Triangle index. Use FindTriangle() to find the triangle at a position.
Random position on the triangle or Zero if the triangle does not exists.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindRandomPointOnNavmesh(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Photon.Deterministic.RNGSession*,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Tries to find a random position on the navmesh using the input position and radius. This can fail when there is no navmesh in range. Using this method instead of will be more costly, but the result is more reliable as it checks more triangles.
member.param: Frame object to use the multi-threaded mailbox optimization
Search a random position inside this radius
member.returns: if a position has been found, if the search failed.
member.remarks: Creates a randomized position first, then tries validate it by finding the closest triangle around the random position, then tries to find any valid near position inside the radius
Creating random points during edit more leads to more efficient and reliable results.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindRandomPointOnNavmesh(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Photon.Deterministic.RNGSession*,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Quantum.MailboxSet8)
member.summary: Tries to find a random position on the navmesh using the input position and radius. This can fail when there is no navmesh in range. Consider using for more reliable results but less performant.
member.param: Search a random position inside this radius
Mailbox to improve reliability while adding performance overhead
member.returns: if a position has been found, if the search failed.
member.remarks: Creates a randomized position first, then tries validate it by finding the closest triangle around the random position.
Creating random points during edit more leads to more efficient and reliable results.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindAnyRandomPointOnNavmesh(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Photon.Deterministic.RNGSession*,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Will return a random point on the navmesh on any of the triangles that intersect the circle weighted by the triangle area. The point can be outside of the radius. It's a good choice to find any point on the navmesh, when it's not important that the point is inside the defined circle.
member.param: Frame object to use the multi-threaded mailbox optimization
member.returns: Returns when a random position was found, otherwise .

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.FindAnyRandomPointOnNavmesh(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Photon.Deterministic.RNGSession*,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Quantum.MailboxSet8)
member.summary: Will return a random point on the navmesh on any of the triangles that intersect the circle weighted by the triangle area. The point can be outside of the radius. It's a good choice to find any point on the navmesh, when it's not important that the point is inside the defined circle. Becomes more accurate (uniform) and performant when setting a mailbox.
member.returns: Returns when a random position was found, otherwise .

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.LineOfSight(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Check line of sight inside the navmesh. This also works in 3D and creates a funnel through the navmesh triangles. Start and destination must be inside the navmesh.

Optionally provide the triangle that the start position is on
Optionally provide the triangle that the destination position is on

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMesh.Raycast2D(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Quantum.NavMeshRegionMask@,Quantum.MailboxSet8,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2@,System.Int32@)

The index of the border that was hit

members.member:
The configuration file for navmesh agent components.
See \ingroup NavigationApi

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.DEFAULT_ID
member.summary: The asset id of the default agent config all agents use when no explicit config is set.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.UpdateInterval
member.summary: The tick interval in which the agent is updated with. The entity index will influence the exact tick the update will happen for each individual entity. 1 = every tick, 2 = every other tick, .. For performance reasons different agents will update at different times although they have the same UpdateInterval.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.PathQuality
member.summary: The quality of the A* heuristic function.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.HeuristicWeight
member.summary: Favor the heuristic over the cost to get to the node to improve the performance of the path finding. Something between 1 and 2 to get a good balance between performance and path quality. Default is 1.

members.member:
The number of waypoints stored on the agent. Default is 6.
The first waypoint is generated at the position of the agent. The last waypoint is only used during the re-pathing transition as soon as the one before the last waypoint is reached. The value is clamped to a minimum of 3 and a maximum of .

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.MaxRepathTimeout
member.summary: The agent automatically performs path finding again when not having reaching the current waypoint in the given time frame (sec). Set to 0 to disable.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.LineOfSightFunneling
member.summary: This option makes sure that unnecessary waypoints (mostly due to region edges in the proximity) are removed when they are applied to the agent component.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.DynamicLineOfSight
member.summary: This setting will make the agent perform a line cast each tick to skip unnecessary waypoints. This is rather expensive try and first.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.DynamicLineOfSightWaypointRange
member.summary: This is similar to the DynamicLineOfSight option but only triggers when the agent is in a certain range of the waypoint.\n Disabled when set to 0.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AutomaticTargetCorrection
member.summary: When an agent destination is selected that is outside the navmesh and if this option is enabled the target position will automatically tried to be corrected and moved to the inside of the navmesh.\n Don't deactive this if using 3D navmeshes.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AutomaticTargetCorrectionRadius
member.summary: When setting a target off the navmesh this value defines the radius that is searched to find a valid position. 0 = target correction disabled 1 = cells inside the radius around the target position are searched. Caveat: searching too many cells can greatly impact the performance. If the search fails or the fallback triangle is used. If greater than 0.25 the value is also used to correct the start position of the agent when selecting a new target.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.EnableWaypointDetectionAxis
member.summary: When using agent rotation speed the agent sometimes will struggle to detect if a waypoint is reached. Use this value to create an perpendicular testing axis in front of the waypoint.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.WaypointDetectionAxisExtend
member.summary: The extend of the perpendicular axis is WaypointDetectionAxisExtend. Should be something around the double radius of the agent.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.WaypointDetectionAxisOffset
member.summary: The offset toward the previous waypoint set by WaypointDetectionAxisOffset.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.DefaultWaypointDetectionDistance
member.summary: Only required when no steering agent is used. The value used to detect if the agent has reached a waypoint in the beginning of the tick. Set this value to agent max speed * delta time.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.MovementType
member.summary: Select how velocity is applied to the agent component.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.VerticalPositioning
member.summary: Only valid when the agents uses 3D transforms. This defines how it's y-position is calculated. Navmesh is default but it has drawbacks because the navmesh geometry might be too simplified and custom tailored. For 3D physics the ground has be created by Quantum colliders.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.Speed
member.summary: The maximum speed of the agent.\n The value can be changed during run-time via the agent component.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AngularSpeed
member.summary: The angular speed of the agent (in Radian/sec).\n Set to 0 to disable agent rotation.\n Set to 200 or more to make its rotation instant-like.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.Acceleration
member.summary: The acceleration of the agent.\n When set to 0 no acceleration is used.\n The value can be changed during run-time via the agent component.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.StoppingDistance
member.summary: The stopping distance of the agent when approaching the target position.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AutoBraking
member.summary: If active the agent will start breaking when approaching the target.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AutoBrakingDistance
member.summary: The distance from the target when the agents starts to brake. Cannot be changed during run-time because the value is cached.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.ClampAgentToNavmesh
member.summary: Only selectable when is set to Transform.\n The setting makes sure that agents are pushed out of invalid navmesh areas similar to what happens with colliders.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.ClampAgentToNavmeshCorrection
member.summary: This is a percentage how much the agent is corrected each tick.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceType
member.summary: Select what type of avoidance procedures are applied to the agent. None will disable the active avoidance but other will still be able to avoid the agent. Remove AvoidanceAgent component to completely disable avoidance.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceQuality
member.summary: Quality settings for agent avoidance.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.Priority
member.summary: The avoidance priority level.
member.remarks: Most important = 0. Least important = 99. Default = 50.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceRadius
member.summary: Radius used for avoidance calculation.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceLayer
member.summary: Mark agent with a layer to prevent agents from avoiding each other.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceMask
member.summary: Other agents whose layer is no in the this mask are ignored.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.MaxAvoidanceCandidates
member.summary: Depending on the quality and number of agents that are influencing each other this value needs to be increased to maintain smooth avoidance.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.ReduceAvoidanceAtWaypoints
member.summary: Activate this to reduce the agent avoidance when getting close to waypoints. Use this only if you have kinematic agents to mitigate agents going off the navmesh. An alternative to this is still under development. Try when using DynamicBodies to mitigate stuck agents on waypoints.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.ReduceAvoidanceFactor
member.summary: This value is multiplied with the agent radius and represents the distance in which the avoidance influence is reduced quadratically.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.AvoidanceCanReduceSpeed
member.summary: This option enables velocity candidates that decrease the agents speed, to make the avoidance maneuvers a bit more natural.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.ShowDebugAvoidance
member.summary: Show avoidance information of velocity obstacles and candidates.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.Loaded(Quantum.IResourceManager)
member.summary: This callback is called after the asset has been loaded. A few values are clamped or calculated and cached.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMeshAgentConfig.OnValidate
member.summary: Validation and clamping is performed by the Unity editor.

member.name: T:Quantum.DynamicAssetDB
member.summary:
A DynamicAssetDB is a database of assets that can be added and removed at runtime. For late joiners, contents are serialized and deserialized using an IAssetSerializer.
All the instances share ownership over assets due to sharing reference counts. is called when an asset is added or replaced, is called when assets' reference count reaches zero due to , , or .

member.summary: Marks a collection field as source for a Quantum heap-based collection. Automatically applied by CodeGen.

member.summary: Marks a field as a reference to a local object. In Unity, a local object is an object from the same scene or prefab. Automatically applied by CodeGen.

member.summary: Marks fields that overlap with other fields in the same struct. Used during memory verification. Applied automatically in unions.

member.name: P:Quantum.Core.FieldOverlapAttribute.Offset
member.summary: Offset of the overlapping field.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.PreserveInPrototypeAttribute
member.summary: Instructs the CodeGen to keep a field/type/enum AS IS in the generated prototype. Can be used to avoid extra proxy types if the simulation type is already Unity-serializable.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.ExcludeFromPrototypeAttribute
member.summary: Instructs the CodeGen to exclude a field/type/enum from the generated prototype. If applied to a type, none of the fields that use this type will be included in the prototype.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.OnlyInPrototypeAttribute
member.summary: Applied on a field prevents it being emit into state object.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.FixedBufferDynamicLengthAttribute
member.summary: Points to a field that contains the length of a fixed buffer in bytes. If used, the CodeGen will generate serialization code that reads/writes the buffer using the specified length, not the entire capacity of the buffer.
member.param: Field that will be used as the buffer's length

member.name: M:Quantum.Core.FixedBufferDynamicLengthAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Points to a field that contains the length of a fixed buffer in bytes. If used, the CodeGen will generate serialization code that reads/writes the buffer using the specified length, not the entire capacity of the buffer.
member.param: Field that will be used as the buffer's length

member.name: P:Quantum.Core.FixedBufferDynamicLengthAttribute.LengthSource
member.summary: Name of the field that contains the buffer's length.

member.summary: Instructs the CodeGen to replace a type in the generated prototype with a different type.

member.summary: Instructs the CodeGen to replace a type in the generated prototype with a different type.

member.name: P:Quantum.Core.ReplaceTypeHintAttribute.Hint
member.summary: Replacement type.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.RequireComponentAttribute
member.summary: If applied on a component type, instructs the CodeGen to emit UnityEngine.RequireComponent attribute on the QPrototype wrapper. If applied on `entity_ref` field, instructs the CodeGen to emit QPrototype wrapper reference instead of QuantumEntityPrototype reference.

member.name: M:Quantum.Core.RequireComponentAttribute.#ctor(System.Type[])
member.summary: If applied on a component type, instructs the CodeGen to emit UnityEngine.RequireComponent attribute on the QPrototype wrapper. If applied on `entity_ref` field, instructs the CodeGen to emit QPrototype wrapper reference instead of QuantumEntityPrototype reference.

member.name: P:Quantum.Core.RequireComponentAttribute.Types
member.summary: Types of required components.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.UnionAttribute
member.summary: Automatically applied to all union types.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.AllocateOnComponentAddedAttribute
member.summary: If applied on a collection field, instructs the CodeGen to allocate the collection when the owning component is added to an entity. The field can belong to the component or to any struct or union nested within (both as a regular field or as a collection's element).
member.remarks: Use together with to avoid memory leaks.
member.param: The initial capacity of the allocated collection.

member.name: M:Quantum.Core.AllocateOnComponentAddedAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: If applied on a collection field, instructs the CodeGen to allocate the collection when the owning component is added to an entity. The field can belong to the component or to any struct or union nested within (both as a regular field or as a collection's element).
member.remarks: Use together with to avoid memory leaks.
member.param: The initial capacity of the allocated collection.

member.name: P:Quantum.Core.AllocateOnComponentAddedAttribute.Capacity
member.summary: The initial capacity of the allocated collection.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.FreeOnComponentRemovedAttribute
member.summary: If applied on a collection field, instructs the CodeGen to free the collection when the owning component is removed from an entity. The field can belong to the component or to any struct or union nested within (both as a regular field or as a collection's element).

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.DontClearOnComponentRemovedAttribute
member.summary: If applied on a collection field, instructs the CodeGen to not nullify it when the owning component is removed from an entity. By default all collection fields are cleared upon component being removed.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.EmitFixedArrayAttribute
member.summary: By default, arrays of integer types are emit as fixed-size buffers, whereas arrays of other types are emitted as . Using this attribute, he CodeGen will emit the array as regardless of the element type.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.DerivedStructAttribute
member.summary: Automatically applied to a struct that uses CodeGen-based inheritance.

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.FormerlyNamedAttribute
member.summary: Applies UnityEngine.Scripting.APIUpdating.MovedFromAttribute attribute to the type. This can preserve value of [SerializeReference] fields that reference an instance of the type. Additionally, if applied on a component, CodeGen will try to reuse the previous .meta file. Note that this attribute can only be applied once, unlike UnityEngine.Serialization.FormerlySerializedAsAttribute.

members.member:
The Frame class is the container for all the transient and static game state data, including the API for entities, physics, assets and others.
The reason for this is that Systems must be stateless to comply with Quantum's predict/rollback model. Quantum only guarantees determinism if all game state data is fully contained in the Frame class. \ingroup FrameClass \ingroup EntityApi

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.FrameBase.EntitiesConfig
member.summary: Global entities configuration

member.name: F:Quantum.Core.FrameBase.EntitiesConfig.CommitCommandsMode
member.summary: When "remove component" and "destroy entity" commands should be committed, effectively cleaning and freeing the data from components' buffers. Both commands still take immediate effect from a semantic perspective, e.g.: checking if the entity exists will return as soon as the "destroy entity" method is called. Commiting commands on will ensure that performing Physics Queries is safe, even after the frame simulation ends (from Unity), but might result in a larger total amount of memory reserved in case many entities are destroyed AND created in the same frame (analogous for "component removal").

member.summary: Check if a destroy is pending for this entity
member.returns: of destroy is pending, otherwise

members.member:
Destroys the entity and all components that were added to it.
The destruction takes immediate effect, i.e., will start to return . The old data, however, will remain in memory until destroy/remove commands are committed, according to the or manually through .
f.Destroy(entity);
\ingroup EntityApi

Creates an entity from a prototype asset. This process is also referred to as "prototype materialization".
\ingroup EntityApi

Creates an entity from a prototype. This process is also referred to as "prototype materialization".
The steps are following: 1. An empty entity is created. 2. For each component prototype: 1. Component prototype is materialized. 2. Component prototype has MaterializeUser invoked. 3. Component prototype is added to the entity. 3. is invoked.
\ingroup EntityApi

Creates (materializes) map prototypes. The difference between this method and calling repeatedly is that fields get resolved and that components are added implicitly.
This is a multi-step process: 1. An empty entity is created for each prototype 2. For each entity-prototype pair: 1. For each component prototype: 1. Component prototype is materialized. 2. Component prototype has MaterializeUser invoked. 3. Component prototype is added to the entity. 2. component is added to the entity. 3. is invoked for each entity-prototype pair.
\ingroup EntityApi

member.summary: Adds (materializes) components to an already existing entity.

members.member:
Adds (materializes) components to an already existing entity. If a component already exists, it will get completely overwritten, but an error message will be logged. To avoid errors in such case use instead.
The steps are following: 1. For each component prototype: 1. Component prototype is materialized. 2. Component prototype has MaterializeUser invoked. 3. Component prototype is added to the entity. 2. is invoked.
if entity does not exist. if any component has been overwritten, otherwise.
\ingroup EntityApi

member.name: T:Quantum.Core.FrameContext

The FrameContext is shared between individual transient instances.
It controls systems like Quantum events, profilers, tasks, culling and subsystems for physics and navigation allocating reused memory.

member.summary: Sorts the elements in the entire list using the specified comparer.

member.name: T:Quantum.ComponentTypeId
member.summary:
Runtime information for component types, containing their ID, size, flags and callbacks.
Internally, types are identified as integers. Number assigned to a type is sequential (starting from 1), hence they are not persistent - any new component type is going to invalidate indices of other types that are registered after it.

member.name: F:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.BuiltInComponentCount
member.summary: The number of built-in components.

member.name: F:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Type
member.summary: All registered component types. Index 0 is reserved for null.

member.name: F:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Size
member.summary: Size of each component type in bytes. Index 0 is reserved for null.

member.name: F:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Flags
member.summary: Flags for each component type. Index 0 is reserved for null.

member.name: F:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Callbacks
member.summary: Callbacks for each component type. Index 0 is reserved for null.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Reset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Resets and allocates collections needed to store component metadata.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.RegisterComponent``1(System.Int32,Quantum.ComponentCallbacks,Quantum.ComponentFlags)
member.summary: Add a component type.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.RegisterComponent``1(System.Int32,Quantum.ComponentSerializeDelegate,Quantum.ComponentChangedDelegate,Quantum.ComponentChangedDelegate,Quantum.ComponentFlags)
member.summary: Add a component type.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentIndex(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns the index of the component type.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the index of the component type based on type's short name, i.e. the name without namespace.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentIndex(Quantum.ComponentTypeRef)
member.summary: Returns the index of the component type based on the type reference.

member.name: P:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Count
member.summary: Returns the number of component types registered plus one.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentSize(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the size of the component type identified by the index.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentType(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the type of the component identified by the index.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentFlags(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the flags of the component identified by the index.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.GetComponentTypeRef(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts the type index to a component type reference. Component type references are persistent and can be used to identify types across different runs and builds.

member.name: T:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Builder
member.summary: Builder for component type registry. This type is not meant to be used directly.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Builder.Add``1(Quantum.ComponentCallbacks,Quantum.ComponentFlags)
member.summary: Add a component type.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Builder.Add``1(Quantum.ComponentSerializeDelegate,Quantum.ComponentChangedDelegate,Quantum.ComponentChangedDelegate,Quantum.ComponentFlags)
member.summary: Add a component type.

member.name: M:Quantum.ComponentTypeId.Builder.Finish
member.summary: Complete building the component type registry.

member.summary: Defines a prototype of a struct of any type.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.StartDisabled
member.summary: If the joint should be materialized with set to , not being considered by the Physics Engine.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.ConnectedEntity
member.summary: A Map Entity that the joint might be connected to. The entity must have at least a Transform2D component prototype.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.Frequency
member.summary: The frequency in Hertz (Hz) at which the spring joint will attempt to oscillate. Typical values are below half the frequency of the simulation.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.DampingRatio
member.summary: A dimensionless value representing the damper capacity of suppressing the spring oscillation, typically between 0 and 1.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.AutoConfigureDistance
member.summary: Automatically configure the target Distance to be the current distance between the anchor points in the scene.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.UseAngleLimits
member.summary: If the relative angle between the joint transform and its connected anchor should be limited by the hinge joint. Set this checkbox to configure the lower and upper limiting angles.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.LowerAngle
member.summary: The lower limiting angle of the allowed arc of rotation around the connected anchor, in degrees.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.UpperAngle
member.summary: The upper limiting angle of the allowed arc of rotation around the connected anchor, in degrees.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.UseMotor
member.summary: If the hinge joint uses a motor. Set this checkbox to configure the motor speed and max torque.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.MotorSpeed
member.summary: The speed at which the hinge motor will attempt to rotate, in degrees per second.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.Joint2DConfig.MaxMotorTorque
member.summary: The maximum torque produced by the hinge motor in order to achieve the target motor speed. Leave this checkbox unchecked and the motor toque should not be limited.

member.name: T:Quantum.Prototypes.PhysicsBodyInertiaMode
member.summary: Enumerates different ways to compute the inertia tensor of a dynamic body.

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.PhysicsBodyInertiaMode.ColliderShape
member.summary: The inertia tensor is computed based on the shape of the entity collider, assuming a uniform distribution of Mass over the collider area (2D) or volume (3D).

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.PhysicsBodyInertiaMode.ParametricShape
member.summary: The inertia tensor is computed based on a specific shape (not necessarily the colliders shape), assuming a uniform distribution of Mass over the collider area (2D) or volume (3D).

member.name: F:Quantum.Prototypes.PhysicsBodyInertiaMode.Explicit
member.summary: The inertia tensor is set to be a value explicitly configured. In this mode, neither the Mass of the body nor the shape of the collider affect the body's inertia.

member.name: T:Quantum.Button
member.summary: The Quantum button used for player input. Instead of using bools or similar data types to represent key presses, the Button type is used inside the Input DSL definition. This is because it only uses one bit per instance, so it is favorable to use where possible. Although they only use one bit over the network, locally they will contain a bit more game state. This is because the single bit is only representative of whether or not the button was pressed during the current frame, the rest of the information is computed locally.

member.name: F:Quantum.Button.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: P:Quantum.Button.IsDown
member.summary: Is the button currently down.

member.name: P:Quantum.Button.WasPressed
member.summary: Was the button pressed this frame.

member.name: P:Quantum.Button.WasReleased
member.summary: Was the button released this frame.

member.summary: Updates the button state. This is called from the code-generated Quantum input methods.
member.returns: The updated button.

member.summary: Casts a button to .

member.summary: Creates a button from the boolean value.
member.param: Creates configured button representing a recently pressed button

member.summary: Creates a subscription. Subscriptions can be cancelled manually by calling , but as long as the derived class overrides and calls periodically, the dispatcher will be free of memory leaks.

Additional flags. Deriving classes can define their own flags starting with 1 << and analyze them in method overrides.
Optional event filter. If returns , handler will not be invoked.

members.member:
Quantum entity reference identifies a unique Entity.
Quantum implements a sparse-set ECS model (similar to enTT). This means entities are just references to collections of components.
Most important advantages: memory consumption is proportional to instantiated entities + ability to add and remove components dynamically. \ingroup EntityApi

member.name: T:Quantum.IQStringUtf8
member.summary: QString interface for UTF-8 strings.

member.name: T:Quantum.QStringUtf8
member.summary: Base class for DSL-generated variable QString(N) fields. QStringUtf8 supports UTF-8. N represents the total size of the string in bytes minus 2 bytes used for bookkeeping. In other words QString(64) will use 64 bytes for a string with a max byte length of 62 bytes, i.e. up to 32 UTF-8 characters.

member.name: T:Quantum.IQString
member.summary: QString interface for UTF-16 strings.

member.name: M:Quantum.IQString.CompareOrdinal(System.Byte*,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Compares a string and a strings byte representation by evaluating the numeric values of the corresponding characters in each string.
member.returns: An integer that indicates the lexical relationship between the two comparands.

member.name: T:Quantum.QString
member.summary: Base class for DSL-generated variable QString(N) fields. QString supports UTF-16 (Unicode in .NET). N represents the total size of the string in bytes minus 2 bytes used for bookkeeping. In other words QString(64) will use 64 bytes for a string with a max byte length of 62 bytes, i.e. up to 31 UTF-16 characters.

member.name: F:Quantum.QString.BytesFieldName

member.summary: Base class for DSL code-generated structs. The fields will be ordered in alphabetical order when the struct is generated.

member.name: T:Quantum.UnionPrototype
member.summary: Base class for DSL code-generated union structs. The union type overlaps in memory the data layout of all the involved structs. Unions can be declared as part of a component. Internally, the union type Data contains the logic necessary for switching between the union types as they are accessed

member.summary: The type of measuring time progressions to update the local simulation.

member.name: F:Quantum.SimulationUpdateTime.Default
member.summary: Internal stopwatch. Recommended for releasing games.

member.name: F:Quantum.SimulationUpdateTime.EngineDeltaTime
member.summary: Engine (Unity) delta time. Useful when pausing the Unity simulation during debugging for example. Caveat: the setting can cause issues with time synchronization when initializing online matches: the time tracking can be inaccurate under load (e.g.level loading) and result in a lot of large extra time syncs request and canceled inputs for a client when starting an online game.

member.name: F:Quantum.SimulationUpdateTime.EngineUnscaledDeltaTime
member.summary: Engine (Unity) unscaled delta time. Different to after using break points this delta time catches up.

member.name: F:Quantum.SimulationUpdateTime.EngineUnscaledCappedDeltaTime
member.summary: Engine (Unity) unscaled delta time that similar to uses UnityEngine.Time.maximumDeltaTime to cap the maximum delta time value. Caveat: the setting can cause issues with time synchronization when initializing online matches: the time tracking can be inaccurate under load (e.g.level loading) and result in a lot of large extra time syncs request and canceled inputs for a client when starting an online game.

Dynamic map currently loaded by the physics engine.
Index of the mesh collider in the .
member.exception: If the is Predicted.
If the is not the same map currently loaded by the Physics Engine.

member.summary: Ensures that the mesh capacity is at least the specified value.

member.summary: Sets a triangle within the used range of a mesh collider.
member.exception: If is out bounds, considering the capacity of this mesh.
If is out of the range of USED triangles.

member.name: M:Quantum.EditMeshScope.TrySetTriangle(System.Int32,Quantum.TriangleCCW@)
member.summary: Try setting a triangle within the used range of a mesh collider.

member.summary: Setting a triangle of a mesh collider without checking the validity of the .

member.summary: Adds a triangle to the mesh collider.
member.exception: If all triangles within the mesh capacity are already being used.
member.remarks: If the mesh collider is at capacity, an InvalidOperationException is thrown.

member.name: M:Quantum.EditMeshScope.TryAddTriangle(Quantum.TriangleCCW@)
member.summary: Tries to add a triangle to the mesh collider.
member.returns: if all triangles within the mesh capacity are already being used. otherwise.

member.summary: Set the triangle used count for this mesh.
member.returns: true if the new is in the range [0, Capacity] and different from the previous count. false otherwise.

member.summary: Removes a single triangle from the mesh collider.
member.param: The index of the triangle to remove.
member.returns: True if the triangle was successfully removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Quantum.EditMeshScope.RemoveTriangles(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes multiple triangles from the mesh collider.
member.param: The starting index of the triangles to remove.
The number of triangles to remove.
member.returns: True if the triangles were successfully removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Quantum.EditMeshScope.GetTriangle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the triangle at the specified index from the edit data in the EditTrianglesScope.
member.param: The index of the triangle to get.
member.returns: The TriangleCCW object at the specified index.
member.exception: If the index is out of bounds for the collider, considering its capacity.

member.name: M:Quantum.EditMeshScope.Dispose
member.summary: Releases the resources used by the EditTrianglesScope.
member.remarks: If the current frame is predicted, the method does nothing. Otherwise, it assigns the EditData to the collider at the specified index in the CollidersRuntimeTriangles dictionary and inserts the mesh collider into the physics engine scene using the TriangleReference obtained from Physics3D.SceneMesh.

members.member:
Used to dynamically edit and manage mesh colliders and their triangles at runtime. This is particularly useful in games with procedurally generated content or user-generated levels, where the environment can change dynamically.
\ingroup Physics3dApi

member.summary: The source map that was used to create this dynamic map.

member.summary: Edits the triangles of a mesh collider within a specific scope. When the scope is disposed, the changes are applied to the map.
member.param: The current Frame. Must be Verified.
The index of the mesh collider to edit.
member.returns: An instance of the EditTriangleScope struct that represents the editing scope.

member.name: T:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData
member.summary: he MapNavMesh.BakeData is a complete mesh that is then further processed and converted to a Quantum navmesh. The mesh is build in the way of a triangle strip.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Name
member.summary: The name of the navmesh accessible inside the simulation by .

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Position
member.summary: The position of the navmesh. Final navmesh vertices are stored in global space and their positions are translated by this during baking.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.AgentRadius
member.summary: The radius of the largest agents that the navmesh is created for. This value is only used to render debug graphics. Older versions of Quantum were permitting different agent radii, but that has been abolished. Now, agents can walk up until their pivot is on the edge of the navmesh. This way the margin agents should keep away from walls is baked into the triangles.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Regions
member.summary: All regions ids that are used in this navmesh. During baking the region ids will be added to the Region list of the Map asset and their index is baked into the navmesh triangles region mask (NavMeshTriangle.Regions). The regions are aggregated on the map because a map can have multiple navmeshes that share the region ids.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Vertices
member.summary: The vertices of the navmesh.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Triangles
member.summary: The triangle of the navmesh. This is a regular mesh data structure where the triangles and vertices are kept in two separate arrays and the triangle points into the vertex array to mark their 3 vertices.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Links
member.summary: Link between positions on the same navmesh.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.ClosestTriangleCalculation
member.summary: The Quantum navmesh uses a grid for spatial partitioning. Each grid cell will have a fallback triangle assigned. The default search is quite slow (BruteForce) while SpiralOut is more efficient it could result in empty fallback triangles.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.ClosestTriangleCalculationDepth
member.summary: The number of grid cells to expand the search.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.EnableQuantum_XY
member.summary: When enabled the navmesh baking will flip Y and Z components of the vertex positions to support navmeshes generated in the XY plane.

member.name: M:Quantum.NavMeshBakeData.Serialize(Quantum.ByteStream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Asset serialization.

member.name: T:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataFindClosestTriangle
member.summary: A fallback triangle for each grid cell is searched. This type symbolized two different search algorithms.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataFindClosestTriangle.BruteForce
member.summary: Iterates through all triangles because the triangle closest can be inside a neighbor cell.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataFindClosestTriangle.SpiralOut
member.summary: Spiral around the current cell to find a closest triangle in neighbors. This could result in empty fallback triangles, which will unlikely cause any problems. Requires to set .

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataFindClosestTriangle.None
member.summary: No fallback triangle search. Will speed up navmesh baking considerably but make be not usable.

member.summary: Creates a extra link between two triangles on the navmesh.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataLink.End
member.summary: End position of the link. Must be on the same navmesh.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataLink.StartTriangle
member.summary: The triangle id of the start.

member.name: F:Quantum.NavMeshBakeDataLink.EndTriangle
member.summary: The triangle id of the end.

member.summary: Can the link be traversed from both directions.

member.name: P:Quantum.NavMeshPathfinder.WaypointCount
member.summary: The number of waypoints currently stored on the agent.
member.remarks: The number of waypoints stored can be lower than the actual computed path (for performance reasons during copying the frame memory).
The first waypoint is always the agent position when the path finding triggered.
The last waypoint is a regular waypoint but a re-pathing is executed when the agent is steering toward it to reduce glitches during path recalculations.

member.name: P:Quantum.NavMeshPathfinder.WaypointMaxCount

Navigation API
\ingroup NavigationApi

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceQuality
member.summary: Quality settings for agent avoidance.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceQuality.Fast
member.summary: Only a minimum of avoidance candidates are computed.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceQuality.Good
member.summary: Reasonable number of candidates are calculated with the best cost-benefit ratio

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceQuality.Best
member.summary: Maximum number of collision free candidates are generated

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceType
member.summary: Avoidance type applied to the navmesh agent.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceType.None
member.summary: Active avoidance disabled. But other agents would still be influenced.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.AvoidanceType.Internal
member.summary: Internal avoidance calculations using velocity obstacle model.

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType
member.summary: Defines how velocity is applied to the agent component.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType.None
member.summary: Not applied.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType.Transform
member.summary: Directly applied to or of the agent.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType.DynamicBody
member.summary: Not used anymore.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType.CharacterController3D
member.summary: Not used anymore.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.MovementType.Callback
member.summary: Does not move the agent but executes the MoveAgent signal.

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.PathQuality
member.summary: The quality of the A* heuristic function.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.PathQuality.Fast
member.summary: Uses parent G and Manhattan Distance

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.PathQuality.Good
member.summary: Creates a pivot point on the entry edge towards goal, recalculates G and Manhattan Distance

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.PathQuality.Best
member.summary: Creates a pivot point on the entry edge towards goal, recalculates G with another pivot point towards start and Euclidean Distance

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.RawPathAllocationStrategy
member.summary: In most cases the maximum raw path size set in will suffice. Exceeding the size in Fixed mode will fail the pathfinding request.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.RawPathAllocationStrategy.Fixed
member.summary: Fixed size allocation. Will fail if the path exceeds the maximum size.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.RawPathAllocationStrategy.Allocating
member.summary: Growing the raw path size when needed.

member.name: T:Quantum.Navigation.VerticalPositioningType
member.summary: Defines how the agents y-position will be calculation in 3D.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.VerticalPositioningType.None
member.summary: Not applied.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.VerticalPositioningType.Navmesh
member.summary: The agents walk on the navmesh.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.VerticalPositioningType.Physics
member.summary: The agents walks on 3D physics geometry.

member.summary: The waypoint is the target

member.summary: The waypoint is the start of a off-mesh link

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.WaypointFlag.LinkEnd
member.summary: The waypoint is the end of a off-mesh link

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.WaypointFlag.RepathWhenReached
member.summary: The agent will perform a repath when reaching the waypoint

member.remarks: Callbacks are always executed from the main thread.

member.summary: Used in 3D to decide whether the actor and target are on the same height. Should be around the height of the character.

member.summary: Used in 3D to quickly filter out agents on other height levels. Should be around the height of the character.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.RawPathSize
member.summary: The default size of the raw path generated by the pathfinder.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.RawPathAllocationStrategy
member.summary: Strategy to execute when paths are attempted with more nodes than . Fixed will fail pathfinding and Allocate will allocate more memory. Default is Fixed.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.EnableAvoidance
member.summary: A global settings to remove all avoidance task scheduling (disable to optimize for performance).

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.AvoidanceRange
member.summary: Defines the distance between two agents (minus their combined radius) used to determine if they are considering each other for further avoidance calculations (broad phase).

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.MaxAvoidanceCandidates
member.summary: A global setting for the maximum number of avoidance candidates used by each agent. More candidates requires more memory and CPU time but can increase the quality.

member.name: F:Quantum.Navigation.Config.VelocityObstacleTruncationFactor
member.summary: The default factor non-moving velocity obstacles are truncated with.

2D Physics API
\ingroup Physics2dApi

Struct to provide thread-safe access to the physics functions.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.ThreadSafeApi.IsValidQueryRef(Quantum.PhysicsQueryRef@)
member.summary: Checks if a given broad-phase physics query reference is valid for result retrieval. Broad-phase queries must be injected before the Physics system Update, their Ref stored and the results retrieved after the Physics Update, on the same frame.
member.param: The query ref to be checked.
member.returns: if the query ref is valid and query results can be retrieved from it this frame. otherwise.

members.member:
Sets which 2D physics collision callbacks will be called for the . The entity must have a PhysicsCollider2D component attached to be able to collide with other physics entries. For receiving the callbacks, set the and implement the corresponding signal on a system.
An entity with a PhysicsCollider2D component attached. The of the desired collision callbacks. frame.Physics2D.SetCallbacks(entity, CallbackFlags.OnDynamicCollision | CallbackFlags.OnStaticTriggerEnter); \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.GetCallbacks(Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary: Get the flags that represent which 2D physics collision callbacks will be called for the . The entity must have a PhysicsCollider2D component attached to be able to collide with other physics entries. To modify the callbacks, use .
member.param: The entity from which to get the callback flags.
member.returns: The callback flags set to a given entity. Default if the entity did not have callback flags set.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.ResetCallbacks(Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary: Resets the tracking history of collisions the might have.

member.summary: Initializes an Api object to provide the physics functions.

member.summary: Initializes the game scene and creates all the physics static entries using the settings from the map.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.Init(Quantum.AssetRef{Quantum.Map})
member.summary: Initializes the game scene by cleaning all physics data and settings and create the new static physics entries for each static collider component in the .
member.param: The Map asset for loading static collider data and other physics settings.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.ResetScene
member.summary: Resets the entries buffer and removes all the dynamic entries of the scene. Also, joints, queries and collision data are cleaned. The is changed to default and the is changed to initial state.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.Update(Quantum.Core.ICollisionCallbacks2D,Photon.Deterministic.FP,Quantum.Task.TaskHandle)
member.summary: Schedules all tasks that compose the 2D Physics Update loop. If the frame map changed, the static part of the physics engine is also reset.
member.param: A listener of physics collision callbacks.
Delta-time of the physics update loop
Handle of the last task scheduled in the graph.
member.returns: The task handle of the last task scheduled by the physics engine.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.ResetMap
member.summary: Resets 2D physics scene map asset guid.

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.Map
member.summary:
Retrieves the map asset currently used on the physics scene.
The map currently used by the physics scene is only updated during verified simulations, so this map can be different from the frame Map during predictions, when the latter is changed.

members.member:
Allocates a persistent on the heap with an initial capacity. To free an allocated persistent collection of 2D hits, use .
The initial buffer capacity allocated for the hit collection. A pointer to the allocated hit collection. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Free a persistent previously allocated on the heap. To allocate a persistent collection of 2D hits, use .
A pointer to the persistent hit collection to be freed. Thrown when the hit collection was not allocated as persistent. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Checks if a given broad-phase physics query reference is valid for result retrieval. Broad-phase queries must be injected before the Physics system Update, their Ref stored and the results retrieved after the Physics Update, on the same frame.
The query ref to be checked. if the query ref is valid and query results can be retrieved from it this frame. otherwise. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Gets the results of a broad-phase query added to the 2D physics scene.
Broad-phase Query reference. Returned when adding a broad-phase query to the physics engine. A with the query hits. \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.summary: Obsolete. Use 'GetQueryHits(in PhysicsQueryRef)' overload instead.

members.member:
Adds a broad-phase raycast query to the 2D physics scene. In order to be resolved, must be added from a system that runs prior to the Physics System.
The ray origin point, in a 2D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. If the query results should return only the closest hit to the ray or all hits (default). A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. The index of the injected query, to be used when retrieving the results with GetQueryHits. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Adds a broad-phase line cast query to the 2D physics scene. In order to be resolved, must be added from a system that runs prior to the Physics System.
The line start point, in a 2D world space. The line end point, in a 2D world space. If the query results should return only the closest hit to the line or all hits (default). A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. The index of the injected query, to be used when retrieving the results with GetQueryHits. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Queries the 2D physics scene with a ray cast and returns the closest hit to the ray origin, if any.
The ray origin point, in a 2D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. A nullable with the closest hit to the ray origin, if any. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Queries the 2D physics scene with a ray cast and adds the closest hit to the ray origin, if any, to a persistent collection of hits.
A previously allocated persistent , to which the query hits will be added. For allocating a persistent HitCollection, see . The ray origin point, in a 2D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Queries the 2D physics scene with a line cast and returns the closest hit to the line start, if any.
The line start point, in a 2D world space. The line end point, in a 2D world space. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. A nullable with the closest hit to the line start, if any. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Queries the 2D physics scene with a shape cast, returning all hits.
The cast start, in a 2D world space. The rotation of the shape, in radians. The 2D shape to be casted. The cast direction and distance, from the cast start point. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. A temporary with the query hits, automatically freed when the current frame simulation ends. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Queries the 2D physics scene with a shape cast, adding all hits to a persistent collection.
A previously allocated persistent , to which the query hits will be added. For allocating a persistent HitCollection, see . The cast start, in a 2D world space. The rotation of the shape, in radians. The 2D shape to be casted. The cast direction and distance, from the cast start point. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The position of the query on the vertical axis, on a 2.5D context. The height of the query from the vertical position on the positive sense of the vertical axis. A temporary with the query hits, automatically freed when the current frame simulation ends. If using vertical position and/or height, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Adds a broad-phase shape overlap query to the 2D physics scene. In order to be resolved, must be added from a system that runs prior to the Physics System.
A 2D transform component, with Position and Rotation info of the shape overlap. A vertical transform component, with Vertical Position and Height info of the shape overlap on a 2.5D context. The 2D shape to be overlapped. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The index of the injected query, to be used when retrieving the results with GetQueryHits. When using a vertical transform, make sure "Use Vertical Transform" is set on the Simulation Config. \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.SetStaticColliderEnabled(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Set a toggleable 2D static collider enabled state to . Disabled static colliders are not considered for collisions or on query resolutions. The static collider state is only changed on Verified frames.
member.param: The index of the collider in the Map StaticColliders2D array.
If the static should be enabled (true) or disabled (false).
member.exception: If the static collider is baked as Immutable.
If the provided is not in the range of static colliders loaded in the current Physics static scene.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics2D.PhysicsEngine2D.Api.IsStaticColliderEnabled(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the current state of a 2D static collider.
member.param: The index of the collider in the Map StaticColliders2D array.
member.returns: if the static is not Immutable and is currently disabled, otherwise.
member.exception: If the provided is not in the range of static colliders loaded in the current Physics static scene.

member.summary: A target entity that this joint might be connected to. If set to , the represents the target anchor position in world space. The connected entity must have at least a component. If it also has a non-kinematic , joint forces are also computed and applied to it.

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Joint.Anchor
member.summary: The anchor offset, in the local space of the joint entity transform. This is the point considered for the joint constraints and where the joint forces are applied.

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Joint.ConnectedAnchor
member.summary: If the joint has a , this represents the anchor offset relative to it, in local space. Else, this is the position in world space considered as target anchor.

member.summary: The upper angle of the allowed arc of rotation around the connected anchor, in radians. Set to enable or disable the angle-limiting constraints.

member.summary: The speed at which the hinge motor will attempt to rotate, in radians per second. Set to enable or disable the hinge motor.

member.summary: The maximum torque produced by the hinge motor in order to achieve the target . Set to in order to allow "unlimited" torque while respecting the FP usable range. Set to enable or disable the hinge motor.

The speed at which the hinge motor will attempt to rotate, in radians per second. If none is provided, i.e. default(NullableFP), and a maximum motor toque is set, the target motor speed will be set to 0. If both motor speed and maximum torque are , is and related constraints will not be applied.

The maximum torque produced by the hinge motor in order to achieve the target . If none is provided, i.e. default(NullableFP), and a target motor speed is set, the maximum torque will be set to . If both motor speed and maximum torque are , is and related constraints will not be applied.

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.SIZE

member.summary: The field of the shape hit by the query.

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.OverlapPenetration
member.summary:
The penetration of a shape overlap query on the collider that was hit.
This field is not computed unless is used in the query options and only applies to Shape Overlap queries. It overlaps in memory and is mutually exclusive with .

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.CastDistanceNormalized
member.summary:
A normalized value (0 to 1) of the feature being casted when the hit occurs, relative to its full cast distance.
This field only applies to Ray, Line or Shape casts. It overlaps in memory and is mutually exclusive with .

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.Point
member.summary: The point in world space where the collider is hit by the query.

member.name: F:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.Normal
member.summary:
The surface normal of the hit.
This field is not computed unless is used in the query options.

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.IsDynamic
member.summary: If the collider hit by the query is not static, i.e. it belongs to an .

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.IsStatic
member.summary: If the collider hit by the query is static.

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.StaticColliderIndex
member.summary: 0-based index of the static collider on the array of the map referenced by . If the hit , this value is -1. Tip: use , , or in order to safely and conveniently access info about a static collider hit.

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.StaticColliderMapRef
member.summary: The reference to the map used by the physics engine when a static collider is hit. Otherwise, this value is default (hit ).

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics2D.Hit.IsTrigger
member.summary: If the collider hit by the query is Trigger.

member.summary: The physics entry index from the entry touched by a the query.
