A component for kinematic character controller prototyping that uses a circle as a shape. You can quickly add this to an entity and set a , then call the method in a system update.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController2D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController2D.BroadPhaseQuery
member.summary: Broad-Phase Query index of the last query added by calling . To use Broad-Phase queries, call before the Physics system Update runs. When Move() is called and a broad-phase query has been added, the method will not perform a new query, but retrieve and use the results of the broad-phase query previously injected.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController2D.MaxSpeed
member.summary: The character's maximum horizontal speed will be clamped by this value.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Velocity
member.summary: The current accumulated velocity of the character controller.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Config
member.summary: Returns the Character Controller config asset.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Grounded
member.summary: Returns true if the character controller is touching the floor.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Jumped
member.summary: If the character jumped this frame. See . This state is consumed in the default steering method .

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController2D.BroadPhaseQueryIndex
member.summary: Obsolete. Use instead.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Init(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.CharacterController2DConfig)
member.summary:
Initializes the 2D character controller component.
Use SetConfig instead in order to set or change the KCC config without resetting other fields. This method is not thread-safe: use the thread-safe overload if calling from a multi-threaded context.
member.param: The KCC asset. If none is provided, the default config asset is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.SetConfig(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.CharacterController2DConfig)
member.summary:
Sets or changes this KCC's config.
This method does not reset other values like and : use Init for that. This method is not thread-safe: use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.param: The KCC asset. If none is provided, the default config asset is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Jump(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Boolean,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FP})
member.summary: Applies a vertical impulse to the KCC velocity. This method is not thread-safe. Use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.remarks: No steering is performed at this stage. To actually move the character, use .
member.param: If the state of the KCC should be ignored.
The amount of impulse applied to the KCC. If none is provided, the of the KCC config is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.AddBroadPhaseQuery(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Adds a Broad-phase query to the 2D Physics scene, according to the KCC config settings, and store the query ref in . This ref will be used on the next time Move or ComputeRawMovement is called to retrieve the resolved query results and perform the KCC logic.
member.remarks: The added BP-Query must be retrieve on the same frame. To cancel the addition of a BP-Query and perform a regular one when moving the KCC, set to default.
member.param: The entity that represents the character. It must have a component, which will be used to compute the query position, along with a (optional), if the entity has one.
A mask that specifies which layers should be considered by the query. If (default), the layer maks defined in the KCC config will be used.
member.returns: The index of the added query.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Move(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Quantum.IKCCCallbacks2D,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FP})
member.summary: Queries the 2D physics scene with , computes a default steering in the desired and moves the . This method is not thread-safe. Use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.remarks: The entity must have a Transform2D component.
member.param: The frame in which the entity is being moved.
The entity that represents the character. Any collider attached by this entity is ignored in the raw movement computation.
An object that can receive the KCC callbacks. If calling from a system that implements the interface, you can use the "this" keyword.

A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against in the raw movement computation. If is passed, the layer maks defined in the KCC config will be used. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property.

If the hit manifold normal should be used when computing the raw movement. If , the normal is computed from the query position and the contact point. If no value is provided, the of the KCC config is used.
The delta timed used to integrate the kcc velocity. If no value is provided, the delta time is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController2D.Serialize(System.Void*,Quantum.FrameSerializer)
member.summary: Serializes a into a .
member.param: The instance into which the struct will be serialized.

member.summary: Queries the 2D physics scene in the position, using the config, and computes movement data. The movement data can be used to apply a custom steering to the character based on its surrounding environment. For using the default steering, use . This method is not thread-safe. Use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.param: The frame in which the physics scene is being queried.
The entity that represents the character. Any collider attached by this entity is ignored in the raw movement computation.
A pointer to a Transform2D component that has the position where the scene will be queried at, usually being the transform.
A pointer to a CharacterController2D component that has the config used by the query, usually being the kcc component.
An object that can receive the KCC callbacks. If calling from a system that implements the interface, you can use the "this" keyword.

A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against in the raw movement computation. If is passed, the layer mask defined in the KCC config will be used. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property.

If the hit manifold normal should be used when computing the raw movement. If , the normal is computed from the query position and the contact point. If no value is provided, the of the KCC config is used.
The 2.5D vertical position where the 2D KCC query will be performed.
The 2.5D vertical height with which the 2D KCC query will be performed.
member.returns: A struct with raw movement data that can be used to compute a custom steering.

members.member:
Adds physics motion to an entity with a driven by the built-in physics systems. You can customize how the movement of the physics body will works by changing the , or . A Kinematic body will not have linear and angular velocities integrated, but those values are still perceived and taken into account when resolving the collision with other dynamic bodies.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

The flags to set up how the physics body will behave in the physics systems.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.Default
member.summary: IsAllowedToSleep | IsAwakenedByForces | ResetInertiaOnAdded | ResetCenterOfMassOnAdded

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.IsKinematic
member.summary: Prevents linear and angular velocities from being integrated.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.FreezeRotation
member.summary: Prevents the physics update to apply changes to the rotation.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.IsAllowedToSleep
member.summary: Allows the physics body to sleep after some time at rest.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.IsAwakenedByForces
member.summary: Allows sleeping physic bodies to be awakened by dynamic interactions.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.IsAwakenedByKinematics
member.summary: Allows sleeping physic bodies to be awakened by kinematic interaction.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.ResetInertiaOnAdded
member.summary: Allows the physics body to compute the inertia automatically when it is added to an entity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.ResetCenterOfMassOnAdded
member.summary: Resets the to the shape when the physics body is added to an entity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ConfigFlags.UseContinuousCollisionDetection
member.summary: Prevents the physics body from passing through other objects when at high velocity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AngularVelocity
member.summary: The rate of change of physics rotation in radians per second.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Velocity
member.summary: The rate of change of physics body position in units per second.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.CenterOfMass
member.summary: The center of mass position relative to the transform's origin.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Force
member.summary: The accumulated forces by the method that will be integrated into delta velocity on the next Physics Update. This value is reset after the integration.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Torque
member.summary: The accumulated torques by the method that will be integrated into delta angular velocity on the next Physics Update. This value is reset after the integration.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Enabled
member.summary: If this physics body component should be taken into consideration by the physics engine. If false, the entity will have as if not having a physics body component.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AllowSleeping
member.summary: Allows the physics body to sleep after some time at rest. When sleeping, it will not integrate the velocity until other forces awaken it. See to setup the time to sleep and the tolerances for linear and angular velocity.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.IsAwakenedByKinematics
member.summary: Allows sleeping physic bodies to be awakened when interacting with other colliders or kinematic bodies.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.IsSleeping
member.summary: Returns true if the object is sleeping.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Drag
P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AngularDrag
P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.FreezeRotation
member.summary: Prevents the physics update to apply changes to the rotation of the physics body.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.UseContinuousCollisionDetection
member.summary:
Prevents the physics body from passing through other objects when at high velocity.
The Continuous Collision Detection uses the GJK algorithm to check the distance between the objects. See to update the settings and improve the collision detection or performance.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Mass
member.summary: The value of the body mass property. If zero, the physics body becomes kinematic body.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.MassInverse
member.summary: The inverse value of the body mass property (1/mass). The inverse mass value is what is stored internally on the component, so accessing or modifying this value directly instead of through the regular can prevent inaccuracies from being introduced in the conversion of values.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.GravityScale
member.summary: The multiplier for the gravity force applied at this physics body.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.InertiaScale
member.summary: A multiplier for the body inertia tensor. When setting a value, the inertia tensor is immediately updated.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.IsKinematic
member.summary: Prevents the physics update integrating the linear and angular velocities of this physics body. Other non-kinematic body in contact with this physics body will still take those velocities into consideration.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.GetInertia
member.summary: Gets the physics body inertia, the resistance to changes in angular velocity.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.GetInertiaInverse
member.summary: Returns the inverse form of the inertia tensor, as it is stored internally.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.SetInertia(Photon.Deterministic.FP,Photon.Deterministic.NullableFP)
member.summary: Sets the inertia tensor value. The greater the value of inertia, the more torque will be required to achieve the same delta angular velocity. This value is automatically recomputed when the value of mass is set.
member.param: The inertia tensor value. This value is scaled by the or by if provided and its inverse value is stored on the component.
If specified, resets the body and immediately applies the new scale to the provided .

members.member:
Creates a dynamic .
The mass of the physics body. The drag of the physics body. The angular drag of the physics body. Allows the physics body to sleep. Allows the physics body to be awaken by an interaction with kinematic bodies. Freezes rotation of the physics body. The center of mass position relative to the transform's origin. The multiplier for the gravity force applied for this physics body. A component. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Creates a kinematic . The mass, drag and angular drag are not considered in kinematic interactions and have 0 as value.
A component. \ingroup Physics2dApi

Initializes a component with custom settings.
The mass of the physics body. The drag of the physics body. The angular drag of the physics body. The used in the physics body. The center of mass position relative to the transform's origin. The multiplier for the gravity force applied for this physics body. A multiplier for the inertia tensor value.
An optional value for the body's inertia tensor. If not specified AND the is set in the , then the inertia tensor will be computed when the body component is added to an entity, based on the shape of the collider, assuming uniform mass distribution.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ResetInertia(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary:
Resets the inertia on this physics body component based on its mass and the shape of a component on the .
This is called automatically when a component is added to an entity, unless the body flag is not set. The body's center of mass affects the computation of the inertia, so consider calling before resetting the inertia, specially if the collider shape has changed. More info on the online docs here.
member.param: The entity from which the collider shape will be retrieved, necessary for computing the inertia.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ResetCenterOfMass(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary:
Resets the of physics body component to the shape of a component attached to the .
This is called automatically when a component is added to an entity, unless the body flag is not set. When changing the collider shape in runtime, consider resetting the center of mass and inertia of the attached physics body, in this order. More info on the online docs here.
member.param: The entity from which the collider component and shape will be retrieved.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddLinearImpulse(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2})
member.summary: Applies the given of linear impulse to the body. The amount of delta-velocity generated by the impulse is inversely proportional to the mass of the body. If the impulse is applied in a that is different than zero, it will also generate a delta angular velocity.
member.param: The impulse vector to be applied.
A vector in world space that represents the distance from the center of mass to the point where the impulse is being applied.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddLinearImpulseAtPosition(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Quantum.Transform2D*)
member.summary: Applies the given linear to the body at a given in world space.
member.param: The impulse vector to be applied.
The point in world space where the impulse is being applied. This position should be withing the collider shape in order to prevent unrealistic behavior.
The current transform of the entity that owns this physics body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddAngularImpulse(Photon.Deterministic.FP)
member.summary: Applies the given of angular impulse to the body. The amount of delta angular velocity generated by the angular impulse is inversely proportional to the inertia of the body.
member.param: The angular impulse to be applied.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ClearForce
member.summary: Clears all the accumulated forces in this body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddForce(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2})
member.summary: Increases the value with the vector. The force is integrated into delta velocity on the next Physics Update. The amount of delta-velocity produced by the accumulated force is directly proportional do the delta-time and inversely proportional do the Mass of the body. If the impulse is applied at a that is different than zero, it will also generate .
member.param: The force vector in world coordinates.
A vector in world space that represents the distance from the center of mass to the point where the impulse is being applied.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddForceAtPosition(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Quantum.Transform2D*)
member.summary: Applies the given to the body at a given in world space.
member.param: The force vector to be applied.
The point in world space where the impulse is being applied. This position should be withing the collider shape in order to prevent unrealistic behavior.
The current transform of the entity that owns this physics body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.ClearTorque
member.summary: Clears all the accumulated torques in this body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.AddTorque(Photon.Deterministic.FP)
member.summary: Accumulates a given of torque, to be integrated into angular velocity on the next Physics Update. The amount of delta angular velocity produced by the angular impulse is directly proportional to the Update delta-time and inversely proportional to the body inertia on each axis.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.GetPointVelocity(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Quantum.Transform2D*)
member.summary: Computes the velocity of the physics body at a given defined in world space.
member.param: The point where the velocity will be computed at, in world space.
The transform component of the entity to which this physics body belongs.
member.returns: A 2D vector representing the computed velocity, also in world space.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.GetRelativePointVelocity(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2,Quantum.Transform2D*)
member.summary: Computes the velocity of the physics body at a given defined on its local space.
member.param: The point where the velocity will be computed at, in local space.
The transform component of the entity to which this physics body belongs.
member.returns: A 2D vector representing the computed velocity, in world space.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.WakeUp
member.summary: Wakes up a sleeping physics body and resets the accumulated time within sleeping thresholds.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Sleep
member.summary:
Puts an enabled physics body into a sleeping state.
If set during a physics collision callback, the body might be awakened by the solver if the collision is not ignored.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody2D.Serialize(System.Void*,Quantum.FrameSerializer)
member.summary: Serializes a into a .

A component that tracks the collisions (trigger and non-trigger) this entity is involved in. The tracked metadata is used to raise physics callbacks according to the callback set.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCallbacks2D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

members.member:
A component with a shape used to represent 2D physics objects in the physics simulation. You can use flags and layers to setup how the object interacts with other colliders.
\ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.Shape
member.summary: The used by the collider.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.Layer
member.summary:
The index of the layer this collider belongs to.
The collision matrix between layers can be configured in the Simulation Config.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.IsTrigger
member.summary: If the collider is trigger.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.Material
member.summary: The asset reference of the physics material used by this collider. See for more details.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.Enabled
member.summary:
If this component should be considered by the Physics Engine.
Disabling a collider has immediate effect: queries will not find it, nor will collisions be solved nor callbacks called. If a collider is re-enabled after the Physics system has already run on this frame, the collider will be re-enabled on the next frame.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsCollider2D.Create(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.Shape2D,Quantum.PhysicsMaterial,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a physics collider component with a .
member.param: The shape to be used by this collider. If the provided shape is a , this collider will create a deep, persistent copy of the provided compound, which will be disposed automatically when this component is removed from an entity.
The physics material asset object. If null, it will use the default physics material.
If the collider is trigger. False by default.
The index of the layer the collider belongs to.
member.returns: An instance of physics collider component.

member.summary: Serializes a into a .

member.summary: The current amount of joints tracked by the component.

member.summary: The number of joints the currently allocated buffer can hold before having to be resized.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.AddJoint(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.Physics2D.Joint@)
member.summary: Adds a to the component. If the buffer is at full , a new buffer will be allocated with double the capacity and existing entries are copied over. In that case, the previous buffer is not disposed immediately and cached pointers are still valid, although outdated.
member.param: The joint that will be added to the component.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.RemoveJoints(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Removes all joints in the component that have a field matching the specified . If none is specified, all joints are removed.
member.remarks: This method does not keep the order of remaining elements in the buffer. To achieve that instead, use and while iterating the buffer in reverse order.
member.param: A numerical tag that can be used to identify a joint or a group of joints. Any joint in the component that matches the tag will be removed. If none is specified, all joints are removed.
member.returns: if at least one joint has been removed.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.RemoveAt(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes the joint at the specified of the component's joints buffer, while keeping the order of the remaining objects. If the remaining elements do not need to be kept in order, is a cheaper option.
member.param: The index of the element to be removed.
member.exception: If the joints buffer is not allocated.
If the is outside the valid range.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.RemoveAtUnordered(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes the joint at the specified of the component's joints buffer. The order of the elements with indices greater than is not kept. If the order must be kept, use instead.
member.param: The index of the element to be removed.
member.exception: If the joints buffer is not allocated.
If the is outside the valid range.

member.param: The allocated buffer for the component joints. if the buffer is not allocated.

member.returns: if the buffer is allocated.

member.summary: Moves to the next joint.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.JointsIterator.Reset
member.summary: Resets the iterator to its original state.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.JointsIterator.CurrentUnsafe
member.summary: A pointer to the current joint being iterated. This allows modifications to the element in the actual buffer of joints.
member.exception: If the iterator buffer is .
If the iterator current index is outside the valid range of the buffer: [0, count).

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.JointsIterator.Current
member.summary: A copy of the current joint being iterated. To modify the actual element in the buffer, use instead.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.JointsIterator.CurrentIndex
member.summary: The index on the buffer of the element currently being iterated. The index is out of the valid range if the iterator has just been created on .

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints2D.GetIterator(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets an auxiliary struct to iterate over the joints in this component. See also and to have direct access to the buffer.
member.remarks: If new elements are added while iterating and this causes the buffer to be expanded, the iterator will keep iterating over the previous buffer, which is not immediately disposed (see ). In this case, further changes to elements in the buffer being iterated will not modify the elements on the new buffer. Hence, the addition of elements while iterating is not recommended, unless the access to the elements are read-only.
member.param: If the buffer elements should be iterated in reverse order. If , allows the removal of elements while iterating without it causing some elements to be skipped.
member.returns: A for the buffer of joints in this component.

members.member:
Info about two entities that were colliding in the 2D Physics.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. See for 2D collisions and for 2D trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo2D.Entity
member.summary: The main entity that was involved in the collision.
member.remarks: The info is taken from the perspective of this entity, which has subscribed to the callback with .
If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities.

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo2D.Other
member.summary: The other entity that was involved in the collision if the was NOT colliding with a static (check ).
member.remarks: If the was colliding with a static, Other has value.

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo2D.IsStatic
member.summary: If the was touching a static collider. If so, has an value and the static collider data can be accessed through .

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo2D.StaticData
member.summary: The of the static collider if the was colliding with a static (check ).

members.member:
Info about a collision between two 2D physics colliders.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. See for 3D collisions and for 2D trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.summary: The collision normal of the manifold.

member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision.

member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision, also accessing the contact normal.
member.param: The current contact point. Default if the iteration is over.
The contact normal. Default if the iteration is over.
member.returns: if there is still a and .

member.name: M:Quantum.CollisionInfo2D.ContactPointIterator2D.NextUnsafe(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2*@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector2*@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision, also accessing the contact normal. The pointers access the fields directly in the collision manifold structure and modifications done to it will affect the collision resolution.

The contact normal. Default if the iteration is over.
If the contact normal is inverted from the perspective of the main entity in the callback.
member.returns: if there is still a and .

members.member:
Info about a collision between a trigger and a non-trigger 2D physics colliders.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. No collision is checked between two kinematic colliders that are both trigger or both non-trigger. No detailed collision data (ContactPoint, ContactNormal and Penetration) is computed for trigger collisions. Use shape queries with the appropriate query options selected as an option. See for 3D trigger collisions and for 2D non-trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics2dApi

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.IgnoreTrigger
member.summary: If the collision should be ignored by the physics engine.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.IsStatic
member.summary: If the is touching a static trigger collider. If so, has an value and the static collider data can be accessed through .

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.Entity
member.summary: The main entity involved in the collision.
member.remarks: The info is taken from the perspective of this entity, which has subscribed to the callback with .
If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.EntityShapeUserTag
member.summary: The of the entity's shape involved in the collision.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.Other
member.summary: The other entity involved in the collision if the is NOT colliding with a static (check ).
member.remarks: If the is colliding with a static, Other has value.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.OtherShapeUserTag
member.summary: The of the other entity's shape involved in the collision. Returns default value if the this is a static collision.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo2D.StaticData
member.summary: The of the static collider if the is colliding with a static (check ).

member.summary: Checks if a given broad-phase physics query reference is valid for result retrieval. Broad-phase queries must be injected before the Physics system Update, their Ref stored and the results retrieved after the Physics Update, on the same frame.

members.member:
Sets which 3D physics collision callbacks will be called for the . The entity must have a PhysicsCollider3D component attached to be able to collide with other physics entries. For receiving the callbacks, set the and implement the corresponding signal on a system.
An entity with a PhysicsCollider3D component attached. The of the desired collision callbacks. frame.Physics3D.SetCallbacks(entity, CallbackFlags.OnDynamicCollision | CallbackFlags.OnStaticTriggerEnter); \ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics3D.PhysicsEngine3D.Api.GetCallbacks(Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary: Get the flags that represent which 3D physics collision callbacks will be called for the . The entity must have a PhysicsCollider3D component attached to be able to collide with other physics entries. To modify the callbacks, use .
member.param: The entity from which to get the callback flags.
member.returns: The callback flags set to a given entity. Default if the entity did not have callback flags set.

member.name: P:Quantum.Physics3D.PhysicsEngine3D.Api.Map
member.summary:
Retrieves the map asset currently used on the physics scene.
The map currently used by the physics scene is only updated during verified simulations, so this map can be different from the frame Map during predictions, when the latter is changed.

members.member:
Adds a broad-phase ray cast query to the 3D physics scene. In order to be resolved, must be added from a system that runs prior to the Physics System.
The ray origin point, in a 3D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. If the query results should return only the closest hit to the ray or all hits (default). A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The index of the injected query, to be used when retrieving the results with GetQueryHits. \ingroup Physics3dApi

Adds a broad-phase line cast query to the 3D physics scene. In order to be resolved, must be added from a system that runs prior to the Physics System.
The line start point, in a 3D world space. The line end point, in a 3D world space. If the query results should return only the closest hit to the line or all hits (default). A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. The index of the injected query, to be used when retrieving the results with GetQueryHits. \ingroup Physics3dApi

Queries the 3D physics scene with a ray cast and returns the closest hit to the ray origin, if any.
The ray origin point, in a 3D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. A nullable with the closest hit to the ray origin, if any. \ingroup Physics3dApi

Queries the 3D physics scene with a ray cast and adds the closest hit to the ray origin, if any, to a persistent collection of hits.
A previously allocated persistent , to which the query hits will be added. For allocating a persistent HitCollection, see . The ray origin point, in a 3D world space. The direction of the ray, not normalized internally. The ray distance from the origin point on the specified direction. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. \ingroup Physics3dApi

Queries the 3D physics scene with a line cast and returns the closest hit to the line start, if any.
The line start point, in a 3D world space. The line end point, in a 3D world space. A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property. The used by the query. If the query results should return only the hit with the smallest or all hits (default). A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against. By default, all layers are considered. By default, all types of colliders are checked against and detailed info (hit point, penetration and normal) is not computed. member.summary: Set a toggleable 3D static collider enabled state to . Disabled static colliders are not considered for collisions or on query resolutions. The static collider state is only changed on Verified frames.

If the static should be enabled (true) or disabled (false).
member.exception: If the static collider is baked as Immutable.
If the provided is not in the range of static colliders loaded in the current Physics static scene.

member.name: M:Quantum.Physics3D.Gjk3D.GetSignedTetrahedronVolume6(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Computes the signed volume, multiplied by 6, of a tetrahedron by calculating the scalar triple product of vectors originating from vertex . To find the actual volume of the tetrahedron, the returned value must be divided by 6. This is not done here because the result is usually used to compute barycentric coordinates by comparing the ratio between two tetrahedra volumes, in which case the x6 cancel each other.

The speed at which the hinge motor will attempt to rotate, in radians per second. If none is provided, i.e. default(NullableFP), and a maximum motor toque is set, the target motor speed will be set to 0. If both motor speed and maximum torque are , is and related constraints will not be applied.

The maximum torque produced by the hinge motor in order to achieve the target . If none is provided, i.e. default(NullableFP), and a target motor speed is set, the maximum torque will be set to . If both motor speed and maximum torque are , is and related constraints will not be applied.

A component for kinematic character controller prototyping that uses a sphere as a shape. You can quickly add this to an entity and set a , then call the method in a system update.
\ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController3D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController3D.BroadPhaseQuery
member.summary: Broad-Phase Query index of the last query added by calling . To use Broad-Phase queries, call before the Physics system Update runs. When Move() is called and a broad-phase query has been added, the method will not perform a new query, but retrieve and use the results of the broad-phase query previously injected.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController3D.MaxSpeed
member.summary: The character's maximum horizontal speed will be clamped by this value.

member.name: F:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Velocity
member.summary: The current velocity of the character controller.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Config
member.summary: Returns the Character Controller config asset.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Grounded
member.summary: Returns true if the character controller is touching the floor.

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Jumped
member.summary: If the character jumped this frame. See . This state is consumed in the default steering method .

member.name: P:Quantum.CharacterController3D.BroadPhaseQueryIndex
member.summary: Obsolete. Use instead.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Init(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.CharacterController3DConfig)
member.summary:
Initializes the 3D character controller component.
Use SetConfig instead in order to set or change the KCC config without resetting other fields. This method is not thread-safe: use the thread-safe overload if calling from a multi-threaded context.
member.param: The KCC asset. If none is provided, the default config asset is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.SetConfig(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.CharacterController3DConfig)
member.summary:
Sets or changes this KCC's config.
This method does not reset other values like and : use Init for that. This method is not thread-safe: use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.param: The KCC asset. If none is provided, the default config asset is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Jump(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Boolean,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FP})
member.summary: Applies a vertical impulse to the KCC velocity. This method is not thread-safe. Use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.remarks: No steering is performed at this stage. To actually move the character, use .
member.param: If the state of the KCC should be ignored.
The amount of impulse applied to the KCC. If none is provided, the of the KCC config is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.AddBroadPhaseQuery(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Adds a Broad-phase query to the 3D Physics scene, according to the KCC config settings, and store the query ref in . This ref will be used on the next time Move or ComputeRawMovement is called to retrieve the resolved query results and perform the KCC logic.
member.remarks: The added BP-Query must be retrieve on the same frame. To cancel the addition of a BP-Query and perform a regular one when moving the KCC, set to default.
member.param: The entity that represents the character. It must have a component, which will be used to compute the query position.
A mask that specifies which layers should be considered by the query. If (default), the layer mask defined in the KCC config will be used.
member.returns: The index of the added query.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Move(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.IKCCCallbacks3D,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FP})
member.summary: Queries the 3D physics scene with , computes a default steering in the desired and moves the . This method is not thread-safe. Use the thread-safe overload if calling from a threaded context.
member.remarks: The entity must have a Transform3D component.
member.param: The frame in which the entity is being moved.
The entity that represents the character. Any collider attached by this entity is ignored in the raw movement computation.
An object that can receive the KCC callbacks. If calling from a system that implements the interface, you can use the "this" keyword.

A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against in the raw movement computation. If is passed, the layer mask defined in the KCC config will be used. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property.

If the hit manifold normal should be used when computing the raw movement. If , the normal is computed from the query position and the contact point. If no value is provided, the of the KCC config is used.
The delta timed used to integrate the kcc velocity. If no value is provided, the delta time is used.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.Serialize(System.Void*,Quantum.FrameSerializer)
member.summary: Serializes a into a .
member.param: The instance into which the struct will be serialized.

member.name: M:Quantum.CharacterController3D.ComputeRawMovement(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef,Quantum.Transform3D*,Quantum.CharacterController3D*,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.IKCCCallbacks3D,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Queries the 3D physics scene in the position, using the config, and computes movement data. The movement data can be used to apply a custom steering to the character based on its surrounding environment. For using the default steering, use .
member.param: The frame in which the physics scene is being queried.
The entity that represents the character. Any collider attached by this entity is ignored in the raw movement computation.
A pointer to a Transform3D component that has the position where the scene will be queried at, usually being the transform.
A pointer to a CharacterController3D component that has the config used by the query, usually being the kcc component.
An object that can receive the KCC callbacks. If calling from a system that implements the interface, you can use the "this" keyword.

A mask that specifies which layers will be checked against in the raw movement computation. If null is passed, the layer maks defined in the KCC config will be used. To create a layer mask, see and overloads on the frame.Layers property.

If the hit manifold normal should be used when computing the raw movement. If , the normal is computed from the query position and the contact point. If no value is provided, the of the KCC config is used.
member.returns: A struct with raw movement data that can be used to compute a custom steering.

members.member:
Adds physics motion to an entity with a driven by the built-in physics systems. You can customize how the movement of the physics body will works by changing the , or . A Kinematic body will not have linear and angular velocities integrated, but those values are still perceived and taken into account when resolving the collision with other dynamic bodies.
\ingroup Physics3dApi

The flags to set up how the physics body will behave in the physics systems.
\ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.Default
member.summary: IsAllowedToSleep | IsAwakenedByForces | ResetInertiaOnAdded | ResetCenterOfMassOnAdded

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.IsKinematic
member.summary: Prevents linear and angular velocities from being integrated.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.IsAllowedToSleep
member.summary: Allows the physics body to sleep after some time at rest.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.IsAwakenedByForces
member.summary: Allows sleeping physic bodies to be awakened by dynamic interactions.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.IsAwakenedByKinematics
member.summary: Allows sleeping physic bodies to be awakened by kinematic interaction.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.ResetInertiaOnAdded
member.summary: Allows the physics body to compute the inertia automatically when it is added to an entity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.ResetCenterOfMassOnAdded
member.summary: Resets the to the shape when the physics body is added to an entity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ConfigFlags.UseContinuousCollisionDetection
member.summary: Prevents the physics body from passing through other objects when at high velocity.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.SIZE
member.summary: The size of the component (or struct/type) in-memory inside the Frame data-buffers or stack (when passed as value parameter). Not related to the snapshot payload this occupies, which is bit-packed and compressed.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.RotationFreeze
member.summary: The that blocks the rotation in the specified axes.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.Velocity
member.summary: The rate of change of physics body position in units per second.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AngularVelocity
member.summary: The rate of change of physics rotation in radians per second for each axis.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.Force
member.summary: The accumulated forces by the method that will be integrated into delta velocity on the next Physics Update. This value is reset after the integration.

member.name: P:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.Torque
member.summary: The accumulated torques by the method that will be integrated into delta angular velocity on the next Physics Update. This value is reset after the integration.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.GetInertiaTensor
member.summary: Returns the inertia tensor based on the stored inverse inertia tensor values.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.GetInertiaTensorInverse
member.summary: Returns the inverse form of the inertia tensor, as it is stored internally.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.SetInertiaTensor(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.NullableFP)
member.summary: Sets the inertia tensor value. The greater the value of inertia on a given axis, the more torque will be required to achieve the same delta angular velocity on that axis. This value is automatically recomputed when the value of mass is set.
member.param: The inertia tensor value. This value is scaled by the or by if provided and its inverse value is stored on the component.
If specified, resets the body and immediately applies the new scale to the provided .

members.member:
Creates a dynamic .
The mass of the physics body. The drag of the physics body. The angular drag of the physics body. Allows the physics body to sleep. Allows the physics body to be awaken by an interaction with kinematic bodies. The rotation freeze flags. The center of mass position relative to the transform's origin. The multiplier for the gravity force applied for this physics body. A component. \ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.CreateKinematic
member.summary: Creates a kinematic . The mass, drag and angular drag are not considered in kinematic interactions and have 0 as value.
member.returns: A component.

members.member:
Initializes a component with custom settings.
The mass of the physics body. The drag of the physics body. The angular drag of the physics body. The used in the physics body. The used in the physics body. The center of mass position relative to the transform's origin. The multiplier for the gravity force applied for this physics body. A multiplier for the inertia tensor value.
An optional value for the body's inertia tensor. If not specified AND the is set in the , then the inertia tensor will be computed when the body component is added to an entity, based on the shape of the collider, assuming uniform mass distribution.
\ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ResetInertia(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary:
Resets the inertia on this physics body component based on its mass and the shape of a component on the .
This is called automatically when a component is added to an entity, unless the body flag is not set. The body's center of mass affects the computation of the inertia, so consider calling before resetting the inertia, specially if the collider shape has changed. More info on the online docs here.
member.param: The entity from which the collider shape will be retrieved, necessary for computing the inertia.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ResetCenterOfMass(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,Quantum.EntityRef)
member.summary:
Resets the of physics body component to the the shape centroid of a component attached to the .
This is called automatically when a component is added to an entity, unless the body flag is not set. When changing the collider shape in runtime, consider resetting the center of mass and inertia of the attached physics body, in this order. More info on the online docs here.
member.param: The entity from which the collider component and shape will be retrieved.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AddLinearImpulse(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3})
member.summary: Applies the given of linear impulse to the body. The amount of delta-velocity generated by the impulse is inversely proportional to the mass of the body. If the impulse is applied in a that is different than zero, it will also generate a delta angular velocity.
member.param: The impulse vector to be applied.
A vector in world space that represents the distance from the center of mass to the point where the impulse is being applied.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AddLinearImpulseAtPosition(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.Transform3D*)
member.summary: Applies the given linear to the body at a given in world space.
member.param: The impulse vector to be applied.
The point in world space where the impulse is being applied. This position should be withing the collider shape in order to prevent unrealistic behavior.
The current transform of the entity that owns this physics body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AddAngularImpulse(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3)
member.summary: Accumulates a given of angular impulse, to be integrated into angular velocity on the next Physics Update. The amount of delta angular velocity produced by the angular impulse is inversely proportional to the body inertia on each axis.
member.param: The angular impulse to be accumulated.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.ClearForce
member.summary: Clears all the accumulated forces in this body.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AddForce(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,System.Nullable{Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3})
member.summary: Increases the value with the vector. The force is integrated into delta velocity on the next Physics Update. The amount of delta-velocity produced by the accumulated force is directly proportional do the delta-time and inversely proportional do the Mass of the body. If the impulse is applied at a that is different than zero, it will also generate .
member.param: The force vector in world coordinates.
A vector in world space that represents the distance from the center of mass to the point where the impulse is being applied.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsBody3D.AddForceAtPosition(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3,Quantum.Transform3D*)
member.summary: Applies the given to the body at a given in world space.
member.param: The force vector to be applied.
The point in world space where the impulse is being applied. This position should be withing the collider shape in order to prevent unrealistic behavior.
The current transform of the entity that owns this physics body.

member.summary: Removes all joints in the component that have a field matching the specified . If none is specified, all joints are removed.
member.remarks: This method does not keep the order of remaining elements in the buffer. To achieve that instead, use and while iterating the buffer in reverse order.
member.param: A numerical tag that can be used to identify a joint or a group of joints. Any joint in the component that matches the tag will be removed. If none is specified, all joints are removed.
member.returns: if at least one joint has been removed.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints3D.RemoveAt(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Int32)

member.param: The index of the element to be removed.
member.exception: If the joints buffer is not allocated.
If the is outside the valid range.

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints3D.RemoveAtUnordered(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Int32)

member.name: M:Quantum.PhysicsJoints3D.GetIterator(Quantum.Core.FrameBase,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets an auxiliary struct to iterate over the joints in this component. See also and to have direct access to the buffer.
member.remarks: If new elements are added while iterating and this causes the buffer to be expanded, the iterator will keep iterating over the previous buffer, which is not immediately disposed (see ). In this case, further changes to elements in the buffer being iterated will not modify the elements on the new buffer. Hence, the addition of elements while iterating is not recommended, unless the access to the elements are read-only.
member.param: If the buffer elements should be iterated in reverse order. If , allows the removal of elements while iterating without it causing some elements to be skipped.
member.returns: A for the buffer of joints in this component.

members.member:
Info about two entities that were colliding in the 3D Physics.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. See for 3D collisions and for 3D trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo3D.Entity
member.summary: The main entity that was involved in the collision.
member.remarks: The info is taken from the perspective of this entity, which has subscribed to the callback with .
If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities.

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo3D.Other
member.summary: The other entity that was involved in the collision if the was NOT colliding with a static (check ).
member.remarks: If the was colliding with a static, Other has value.

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo3D.IsStatic
member.summary: If the was touching a static collider. If so, has an value and the static collider data can be accessed through .

member.name: P:Quantum.ExitInfo3D.StaticData
member.summary: The of the static collider if the was colliding with a static (check ).

members.member:
Info about a collision between two 3D physics colliders.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. See for 2D collisions and for 3D trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: T:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D
member.summary: An iterator over multiple triangles collisions with a given mesh.

member.name: P:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.Count
member.summary:
The number of contact points on this collision.
If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this is the sum of all contact points with the triangles of the mesh. To access each specific triangle collision, check .

member.name: P:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.Average
member.summary:
The average contact point on this collision.
If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this is the average of all contact points with the triangles of the mesh. To access each specific triangle collision, check .

member.name: P:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.First
member.summary: The first contact point computed on this collision. Contact points are computed in NO particular order. If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this is the first contact point with the first triangle.

member.name: P:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.Current
member.summary: The current contact point iterated. must have been called at least once and must have returned true in the latest call. Contact points are computed in NO particular order.

member.name: P:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.ContactNormal
member.summary: The collision normal of the manifold.

member.name: M:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.Next(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision. If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this will iterate through all contact points in all colliding triangles.
member.param: The current contact point. Default if the iteration is over.
member.returns: if there is still a .

member.name: M:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.Next(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3@)
member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision, also accessing the contact normal. If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this will iterate through all contact points and normals in all colliding triangles.
member.param: The current contact point. Default if the iteration is over.
The contact normal. Default if the iteration is over.
member.returns: if there is still a and .

member.name: M:Quantum.CollisionInfo3D.ContactPointIterator3D.NextUnsafe(Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3*@,Photon.Deterministic.FPVector3*@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Iterates through the contact points of a collision, also accessing the contact normal. The pointers access the fields directly in the collision manifold structure and modifications done to it will affect the collision resolution. If the entity is colliding with a mesh, this will iterate through all contact points and normals in all colliding triangles.
member.param: The current contact point. Default if the iteration is over.
The contact normal. Default if the iteration is over.
If the contact normal is inverted from the perspective of the main entity in the callback.
member.returns: if there is still a and .

Distance from the center of the capsule to the top of the internal cylinder. This is NOT the capsule Height.
Height = 2 * (Radius + Extent) Extent = (Height / 2) - Radius
Offset of the center of the capsule relative to its collider or query position.
Rotation offset of the capsule relative to its collider or query rotation.

members.member:
Info about a collision between a trigger and a non-trigger 3D physics colliders.
The info is taken from the perspective of the entity that has subscribed to the callback with . If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities. No collision is checked between two kinematic colliders that are both trigger or both non-trigger. No detailed collision data (ContactPoint, ContactNormal and Penetration) is computed for trigger collisions. Use shape queries with the appropriate query options selected as an option. See for 2D trigger collisions and for 3D non-trigger collisions. \ingroup Physics3dApi

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.IgnoreTrigger
member.summary: If the collision should be ignored by the physics engine.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.IsStatic
member.summary: If the is touching a static trigger collider. If so, has an value and the static collider data can be accessed through .

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.Entity
member.summary: The main entity involved in the collision.
member.remarks: The info is taken from the perspective of this entity, which has subscribed to the callback with .
If both entities involved in a collision have subscribed to this collision type callback, it will be called twice, each time carrying information from the perspective of one of the entities.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.EntityShapeUserTag
member.summary: The of the entity's shape involved in the collision.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.Other
member.summary: The other entity involved in the collision if the is NOT colliding with a static (check ).
member.remarks: If the is colliding with a static, Other has value.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.OtherShapeUserTag
member.summary: The of the other entity's shape involved in the collision. Returns default value if the this is a static collision.

member.name: P:Quantum.TriggerInfo3D.StaticData
member.summary: The of the static collider if the is colliding with a static (check ).

members.member: \defgroup PhysicsCommonApi Physics Common API \brief Groups the Physics types that are relevant for both 2D and 3D physics engines.

member.name: T:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.ConstraintsSolverType
member.summary: Defines which solver algorithm should be used by the physics engine when solving velocity and position constraints.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.ConstraintsSolverType.Default
member.summary: Uses the default solver for the current version. For v3.1 and up, this is the ProjectedGaussSeidel solver.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.ConstraintsSolverType.HybridJacobiGaussSeidel
member.summary: Solver introduced on v2.1 that has an improved parallelization relative to the legacy Gauss-Seidel solver used on v2.0. It is expected to have a better performance if the simulation is running in multiple threads. It is recommended to use the ProjectedGaussSeidel solver unless you have a legacy project that needs to use the old solver.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.ConstraintsSolverType.LegacyGaussSeidel
member.summary: Legacy solver from v2.0. It is recommended to use the ProjectedGaussSeidel solver unless you have a legacy project that needs to use the old solver.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.ConstraintsSolverType.ProjectedGaussSeidel
member.summary: New default solver for 3.1 and up. Traditional interactive Gauss-Seidel with positive aggregate clamp, but allowing single-pass negative impulse.

members.member:
Global physics settings which are part of the .
\ingroup PhysicsCommonApi

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.Gravity
member.summary: Define the gravity of the physics simulation. For a 2D world set the y-component to zero. Default is (0, -10, 0).

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.SolverType
member.summary: Defines which solver algorithm will be used by the Physics Engines.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.WarmStart
member.summary: When using Projected Gauss Seidel, whether to apply the previous accumulated impulses as initial step.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.SolverIterations
member.summary: The physics collision solver runs in sub-steps to improve quality. Default is 4.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.PositionSolverIterations
member.summary: The physics position constraint solver (used on Joint, for instance) runs in sub-steps to improve quality. Default is 2.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.UseJoints
member.summary: Enable the use of joints in the physics engines. If joints are not used, this can be disabled to reduce overhead. Default is .

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.UseAngularVelocity
member.summary: Lock the angular velocity for dynamic bodies. Disabling this option leads not only to faster but also to more stable physics simulation for games that can use it. Default is .

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.UseVerticalTransform
member.summary: Toggle the use of a vertical transform component for 2.5D worlds that have a simplified concept of height. Default is .

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.PenetrationAllowance
member.summary: Allow a certain degree of penetration to improve the stability of the physics simulation. Default is 0.01.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.PenetrationCorrection
member.summary: How much of the exceeding penetration (above allowance) should be corrected in a single frame. 0 = no correction, 1 = full correction. Default is 0.5.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.MinLinearIntegration
member.summary: A physics body's linear velocity component (x, y or z, the latter only in the 3D Physics) is rounded to 0 if its absolute value is smaller than this minimum linear integration. A small amount can increase the physics simulation stability and improve sleep detection. Default is 0.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.MinAngularIntegration
member.summary: A physics body's angular velocity component (x, y or z in the 3D Physics and the amount relative to the vertical axis in the 2D Physics) is rounded to 0 if its absolute value is smaller than this minimum angular integration. A small amount can increase the physics simulation stability and improve sleep detection. Default is 0.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.MinContactInverseMass
member.summary: When Physics Bodies have a very large Mass or Inertia, the inverse value of the perceived mass in contact points as computed by the Physics Solver might get very small and close to zero. Under such conditions, rounding errors become very significant and this can often be translated into impulse spikes. This setting functions as a minimum threshold to this inverse mass value, below which the value is clamped. Set to 0 to never clamp.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.SimplexMinBitShift
member.summary: Scales the GJK Voronoy Simplex space by shifting points' raw values. Consider increasing if working with small-scaled shapes. Default is 0.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.SimplexMaxBitShift
member.summary: The GJK Voronoy Simplex space is scaled by progressively by shifting points' raw values up to this max value when the current instance becomes affine dependent, avoiding degenerate cases due to limited precision. Default is 2.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.ShapeCastMaxIterations
member.summary: The max number of iterations performed by the GJK algorithm while searching for a solution below . Default is 16.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.ShapeCastMinIterations
member.summary: The minimum number of iterations performed by the GJK algorithm if the query option is enabled.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.ShapeCastHardTolerance
member.summary: A GJK iteration result below this threshold is acceptable as a finishing condition. In general, increasing the hard tolerance decreases the accuracy of the result, but reduces computational cost by requiring less iterations. Default is 0.01.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.ShapeCastSoftTolerance
member.summary: A GJK resolution that fails to find an acceptable result below after will still return positive if the best solution found is below this soft threshold. Default is 0.05.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.AllowSleeping
member.summary: Toggles the detection of physics bodies in a sleeping state. If allowed, the simulation will skip forces and velocity integrations and collisions detection on physics bodies while they are asleep. Default is .

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.SleepTimeSec
member.summary: For how many seconds a physics body should stay under the linear and angular velocity sleeping thresholds before being put in a sleeping state. Default is 0.5.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.LinearSleepTolerance
member.summary: Amount of linear velocity below which a physics body might be considered as standing still for sleeping detection. Default is 0.02.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.AngularSleepToleranceInRad
member.summary: Amount of angular velocity below which a physics body might be considered as standing still for sleeping detection.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.AllowCCD
member.summary: Allows CCD to be performed if the Physics Body has CCD enabled on its Config flags. Default is .

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.CCDLinearVelocityThreshold
member.summary: If CCD is allowed, it will be performed on all Physics Bodies that have it enabled and have a linear velocity magnitude above this threshold. Default is 3.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.CCDAngularVelocityThreshold
member.summary: If CCD is allowed, it will be performed on all Physics Bodies that have it enabled and have a angular velocity magnitude above this threshold. Default is 3.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.CCDDistanceTolerance
member.summary: The absolute distance value below which the Physics Bodies under CCD check can be considered as touching. Default is 0.001.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.MaxTimeOfImpactIterations
member.summary: The maximum number of iterations performed by the CCD algorithm when computing the time of impact between two Physics Bodies. Default is 16.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.MaxRootFindingIterations
member.summary: The maximum number of iterations performed when computing the point in time when the distance between two Physics Bodies in a given separation axis is below the tolerance. Default is 32.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultEntriesCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of supported entities when a scene is initialized. Memory for more entities will be allocated if necessary. The smallest value is 32.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultStaticsCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of supported static entities when a scene is initialized. Memory for more entities will be allocated if necessary. The smallest value is 64.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultQueriesCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of supported broad-phase queries per frame when a scene is initialized. Memory for more queries will be allocated if necessary. The smallest value is 64.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultQueryItemsCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of query manifolds for collision interactions per bucket when a scene is initialized. Memory for more manifolds will be allocated if necessary. The default value is 1024.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultHitCollectionItemsCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of memory allocated for items in the . The default value is 32.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultBucketsCapacity
member.summary: The initial amount of buckets used in the physics broad phase to solve collisions in parallel. Use a reasonable amount according to how many colliders you have. The default value is 16.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultIslandsCapacity
member.summary: The default number of islands of entities that are in contact in the physics update. Memory for more islands will be allocated if necessary. The smallest value is 16.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultManifoldsCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of manifolds for collision interactions per bucket when a scene is initialized. Memory for more manifolds will be allocated if necessary. The default value is 1024.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultJointsCapacity
member.summary: The initial number of supported joints when a scene is initialized. Memory for more joints will be allocated if necessary. The default value is 128. This value can be set to 0 if not using Joints.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultPhysicsMaterial
member.summary: The default asset used when initializing a 2D or 3D physics collider component with no other material specified

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultCharacterController2D
member.summary: The default asset used when initializing a CharacterController2D component with no other config specified

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.DefaultCharacterController3D
member.summary: The default asset used when initializing a CharacterController3D component with no other config specified

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.Layers
member.summary: The collision layer names recognized by the Quantum simulation. Can be imported from Unity layers on the editor.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.Config.LayerMatrix
member.summary: The collision matrix of the layers recognized by the Quantum simulation. Can be imported from Unity's layer matrix on the editor.

member.name: T:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.StaticColliderMutableMode
member.summary: Defines if the static collider can be toggled on and off at runtime.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.StaticColliderMutableMode.Immutable
member.summary: The collider cannot be enabled or disabled at runtime.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.StaticColliderMutableMode.ToggleableStartOn
member.summary: The collider can be toggled at runtime and starts enabled.

member.name: F:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.StaticColliderMutableMode.ToggleableStartOff
member.summary: The collider can be toggled at runtime and starts disabled.

member.name: T:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.BucketAxis
member.summary: The world axis setting for divide the physics entries in different buckets.

member.name: T:Quantum.PhysicsCommon.SortAxis
member.summary: The sorting axis for the queries in a bucket that are sorted according to their position.
